title: link_ctx.link_key not explicitly zeroized on lichen_link_cleanup()
description:
In lichen_l2.c:274, lichen_link_cleanup(&link_ctx) is called to clean up the link context. The cleanup function is external (from <lichen/link_ctx.h>), so its implementation must be verified to ensure it zeroizes link_ctx.link_key.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:274

If lichen_link_cleanup() does not explicitly clear the link_key field (and any other key material in link_ctx), the key persists in static memory after disable. This static memory (link_ctx at file scope) remains allocated for the lifetime of the process.

Impact: If link_cleanup() fails to zeroize, link keys remain in memory after the L2 interface is disabled, violating key lifecycle management. Keys should be wiped when no longer needed.

Recommendation: Audit lichen_link_cleanup() implementation to confirm it zeroizes all key material. If it does not, add explicit memset_s/explicit_bzero for link_key after the cleanup call.
